bb48447738c52714.title: Matrix mismatch: 7 port(s) in matrix but not in use; 1 port(s) open but missing endpointslice
bb48447738c52714.ai_summary_short:
---

**TL;DR:** CRI-O's ephemeral port (36239) is open but has no EndpointSlice and is not filtered after PR #400 removed Linux dynamic ranges.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: CRI-O ephemeral port not filtered by EPS validation test** (affects Tests: `[It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [FAIL] Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports
    /tmp/secret/commatrix/test/e2e/validation_test.go:161
    ```
    `Port 36239: LISTEN 0 4096 10.0.1.66:36239 0.0.0.0:* users:(("crio",pid=2709,fd=12))`
    `matrix-diff-ss: - Ingress,TCP,36239,,crio,,,master,false`
  - **Root Cause:** CRI-O (the container runtime, PID 2709) opens a randomly-assigned ephemeral port (36239, in the Linux range 32768–60999) that has no Kubernetes EndpointSlice. `validation_test.go:159` calls `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges(missingEPSMat, commatrix.DynamicRanges)`, but `commatrix.DynamicRanges` is populated by `GetDynamicRanges()` (`pkg/dynamic-ranges/dynamic-ranges.go`) which returns only the Kubernetes NodePort range (30000–32767). Port 36239 > 32767, so it is not filtered. Prior to PR #400 (`7d56343`), the Linux ephemeral range was included in `DynamicRanges` and this port would have been filtered; that PR removed it to prevent the firewall matrix from leaving all ephemeral ports open, but also broke the EPS validation filter.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** `1fec55d` Merge pull request #400 from shirmoran/remove-linux-dynamic-range (`7d56343` "Remove Linux Dynamic ranges from generated communication matrix")
  - **Is it a flake?** No — CRI-O consistently opens an ephemeral port above 32767 on every cluster, so this failure will recur on every run until fixed.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Add `"crio"` to the `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` allowlist in `test/e2e/validation_test.go` (alongside the existing `rpc.statd` filter), since CRI-O is a host-level process that opens random ephemeral ports with no EndpointSlice by design. Alternatively, add a separate Linux ephemeral range filter (32768–60999) to the EPS validation check without putting it back into the generated firewall matrix.
  - **Affected tests:** `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports`

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause. The 7 stale matrix entries (TCP 80, 443, 8798, 9099, 9192, 9443, 10258 — documented or EPS-generated but not actually open on the SNO node) generate only `logrus.Warning` calls (lines 108–110 and 153–155 of `validation_test.go`) and do not fail any test. They indicate the `docs/stable/raw/aws-sno.csv` documented matrix is drifting from actual OCP 4.23 SNO port usage but are not the direct failure cause.

**Per-Version Notes:**
- OCP 4.23 (evidence): CRI-O ephemeral port 36239 fails the EPS validation; 7 stale matrix entries logged as warnings.
- OCP 5.0 (no evidence file): Same root cause expected — identical `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges` logic and `GetDynamicRanges` returning only NodePort range; CRI-O ephemeral port behavior unchanged.

**Affected Images:** `ci/telco-runner:latest` (test runner); OCP release `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.23.0-0.nightly-2026-06-24-155843`

**Overall Issue Class:** `matrix_mismatch`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH

f27112558ab43e17.title: Matrix mismatch: 7 port(s) in matrix but not in use; 33 port(s) open but missing endpointslice
f27112558ab43e17.ai_summary_short:
---

**TL;DR:** CRI-O ephemeral TCP ports lack EndpointSlice entries and are not filtered, causing the matrix-vs-ss validation test to fail.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: CRI-O ephemeral TCP ports pass through all filters unblocked** (affects Test: `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports` — `test/e2e/validation_test.go:161`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [FAILED] the following ports are used but don't have an endpointslice:
      Ingress,TCP,40803,,crio,,,master,false
      Ingress,TCP,40999,,crio,,,master,false
      Ingress,TCP,44465,,crio,,,master,false
      Ingress,TCP,39127,,crio,,,worker,false
      Ingress,TCP,41873,,crio,,,worker,false
      Ingress,TCP,44687,,crio,,,worker,false
    ```
    From `build-log.txt:180-187` and `matrix-diff-ss` (all six `-` prefixed crio entries).
  - **Root Cause:** CRI-O binds ephemeral TCP ports (32768–60999 range) on both master and worker nodes at runtime; these have no Kubernetes EndpointSlice. Two filters protect against spurious failures: `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` (excludes `rpc.statd`, `dnsmasq`, `dig` — but **not** `crio`) and `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges` (excludes ports within `commatrix.DynamicRanges` — but commit `7d56343` intentionally removed the Linux ephemeral range from the generated commatrix). With both escape hatches closed for `crio`, the six ports reach the hard `Fail()` at `validation_test.go:161`. The first domino is that commit `6cf4613` added `rpc.statd`/`dnsmasq`/`dig` to the skip list but omitted `crio`.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Likely the same PR that introduced commit `6cf4613` ("Skip rpc.statd, dnsmasq and dig port in validation test"), which added the exclusion list in `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` but missed the `crio` service. The removal of Linux ephemeral ranges (commit `7d56343` / `fec55d`) is the upstream enabler.
  - **Is it a flake?** Partially — the specific port numbers change every run (ephemeral allocation), but CRI-O consistently opens several TCP ports in this range, so the failure reproduces reliably.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Add `crio` to the skip list in `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` (`test/e2e/validation_test.go:221`), mirroring the `rpc.statd` treatment. Alternatively, add `crio` ephemeral ports as static entries with a note that they are host-level and not backed by a Service.
  - **Affected tests:** `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports`

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single group. The 7 stale matrix entries (TCP 6388, 8798×2, 9192, 9443; UDP 68×2) are reported only as `level=warning` by `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices`'s sibling code path and do **not** cause a hard failure; they are documentation debt but not the breaking issue.

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 4.23 nightly (2026-06-24-155843) on baremetal OVN — CRI-O ephemeral port behavior is consistent with any recent 4.x release; this failure would affect any version where the Linux dynamic range was removed from the commatrix and `crio` was not added to the service skip list.

**Affected Images:** `release-4.23` branch of `github.com/openshift-kni/commatrix` (oc-commatrix binary built in-job); no external container image regression.

**Overall Issue Class:** `matrix_mismatch`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH

9ba5ead2f0fb6a59.title: Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports
9ba5ead2f0fb6a59.ai_summary_short:
---

**TL;DR:** CRI-O ephemeral port 44697 lacks an EndpointSlice and escapes dynamic-range filtering after PR #400 removed Linux ephemeral ranges.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Ephemeral crio port passes EndpointSlice validation filter** (affects Tests: `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports`, `TestE2e`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    Fail: "the following ports are used but don't have an endpointslice: Ingress,TCP,44697,,crio,,,master,false"
    # raw-ss-tcp:
    LISTEN 0      4096   192.168.17.15:44697 0.0.0.0:* users:(("crio",pid=6365,fd=12))
    # port-map.txt:
    PORT 44697: Issues: NO_ENDPOINTSLICE; Ephemeral range: YES
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** CRI-O binds a randomly-assigned OS ephemeral port (44697, within Linux range 32768–60999) to the node IP. The test at `validation_test.go:161` calls `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges(missingEPSMat, commatrix.DynamicRanges)` (line 159), but `commatrix.DynamicRanges` now only contains the Kubernetes NodePort service range — it no longer includes the Linux ephemeral range. Commit `7d56343` (PR #400) explicitly removed Linux dynamic ranges from the generated matrix, so port 44697 is no longer excluded and triggers `Fail()`. The 4 stale matrix entries (TCP 8798, 9192, 9443, UDP 68) are logged as warnings only and do not cause test failure.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** `7d56343` — "Remove Linux Dynamic ranges from generated communication matrix" (PR #400, `openshift-kni/commatrix`)
  - **Is it a flake?** Yes — the ephemeral port number changes on every crio restart/reboot, so the test may pass when crio happens to pick a port not in the matrix or a lower-numbered port.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Either re-add the Linux ephemeral range (32768–60999) specifically to the `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges` call at `validation_test.go:159`, or add `crio` to `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices()` similar to how `rpc.statd` is already skipped.

96a13234ae77f068.title: Matrix mismatch: 7 port(s) in matrix but not in use; 6 port(s) open but missing endpointslice
96a13234ae77f068.ai_summary_short:
Now I have enough to write the complete analysis.

---

**TL;DR:** Crio ephemeral ports on 5 nodes fail the EndpointSlice check because dynamic-range filtering was removed.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: crio ephemeral ports have no EndpointSlice and are no longer filtered** (affects Test: `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [FAILED] the following ports are used but don't have an endpointslice:
     Ingress,TCP,44829,,crio,,,master,false
     Ingress,TCP,45195,,crio,,,master,false
     Ingress,TCP,45913,,crio,,,master,false
     Ingress,TCP,37363,,crio,,,worker,false
     Ingress,TCP,39509,,crio,,,worker,false
     Ingress,TCP,40519,,crio,,,worker,false
    ```
    From `raw-ss-tcp`: `LISTEN 0 4096 192.168.111.20:44829 0.0.0.0:* users:(("crio",pid=4426,fd=12))` — confirmed as crio process on node IP, not a Kubernetes Service, so no EndpointSlice exists.
  - **Root Cause:** Commit `7d56343` (PR #400, "Remove Linux Dynamic ranges from generated communication matrix", Feb 5 2026) stripped `DynamicRanges` from the generated commatrix. The test at `validation_test.go:159` calls `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges(missingEPSMat, commatrix.DynamicRanges)` — but `commatrix.DynamicRanges` is now empty, so the filter is a no-op. Crio binds to random ephemeral ports (32768–60999) on each node IP, which previously fell inside the dynamic range and were silently filtered. `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` at line 157 also does not handle `crio` (only `rpc.statd` and `dnsmasq`). Both filters now fail to exclude these ports, exposing the regression.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** `7d56343` / PR #400 — `https://github.com/openshift-kni/commatrix/pull/400`
  - **Is it a flake?** No — crio always binds ephemeral ports on startup; the specific port numbers vary per run but the failure reproduces on every job.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Add `"crio"` to the `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` allowlist in `test/e2e/validation_test.go:221–239`, or restore ephemeral-range filtering specifically for the EndpointSlice check (separate from the matrix generation path). Alternatively, investigate whether crio's gRPC stream-server ports should carry a static `ComDetails` entry so they match an EndpointSlice.
  - **Affected tests:** `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports` (`validation_test.go:141`)

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause group. The 7 stale matrix entries (`6388`, `8798` ×2, `9192`, `9443`, `UDP/68` ×2) only produce log warnings (`logrus.Warningf` at line 154) and do not contribute to the test failure; they represent documentation drift independent of the crio regression.

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 5.0 (nightly 2026-06-22-235733) — failure is deterministic on this version due to missing dynamic-range filtering for crio ephemeral ports.

**Affected Images:** `ci/telco-runner:latest` (test runner), `ocp-kni/test:dev-scripts` (cluster setup); no image pull errors — all images pulled successfully.

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_regression`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH

2292fd7c7af55650.title: telco5g-cluster-setup
2292fd7c7af55650.ai_summary_short:
---

**TL;DR:** CI infra failure — no free telco bare-metal cluster available; commatrix tests never ran.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: CI infrastructure — no free cluster lease** (affects Tests: telco5g-cluster-setup, telco5g-log-gather, telco5g-release-cluster)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    "stderr": "Failed to find a free cluster!",
    "stderr_lines": ["Failed to find a free cluster!"]
    fatal: [XXXXXXXXXXXX]: FAILED! => { "cmd": ["python3", "~/telco5g-lab-deployment/scripts/upstream_cluster_all.py", "--get-cluster", "-e", "upstreambil", "--exclude", "cnfdu1", "--exclude", "cnfdu3"], "rc": 1 }
    Step telco5g-network-flow-matrix-bm-telco5g-cluster-setup failed after 22s.
    Cluster doesn't exist, job failed, no need to run gather
    Cluster doesn't exist, job failed
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `telco5g-cluster-setup` pre-phase step invoked `upstream_cluster_all.py --get-cluster -e upstreambil --exclude cnfdu1 --exclude cnfdu3` on the bastion host. The script returned `"Failed to find a free cluster!"` with rc=1, meaning the telco bare-metal cluster pool (upstreambil environment) had no available slot. The commatrix tests never started. The `telco5g-log-gather` and `telco5g-release-cluster` failures are direct cascades — their scripts explicitly check for cluster existence and exit immediately when none was allocated.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — this is a CI resource contention issue, not a code change.
  - **Is it a flake?** Yes — cluster pool exhaustion is transient and resolves when other jobs finish and return their leases.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Retry the job. If it recurs frequently, investigate whether the `upstreambil` pool size is sufficient for the current job load or whether other long-running jobs are holding clusters.
  - **Affected tests:** telco5g-cluster-setup, telco5g-log-gather, telco5g-release-cluster

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause — all three failures are one cascade chain: cluster not provisioned → log-gather and release-cluster both exit immediately with "Cluster doesn't exist."

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 5.0 nightly (5.0.0-0.nightly-2026-06-22-235733) — no OCP-version-specific issue; the cluster was never provisioned so OCP version is irrelevant.

**Affected Images:** None — no image pull failures. `ci/telco-runner:latest` and `release:latest` (192 images) imported successfully before failure.

**Overall Issue Class:** infra_quota

**Overall Severity:** MEDIUM

c8887cfbeba86a64.title: Cluster in error state (must-gather triggered)
c8887cfbeba86a64.ai_summary_short:
Now I have everything I need. 

---

**TL;DR:** CI builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: CI builder image unavailable (`manifest unknown`)** (affects: deployment setup / `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet wait timeout)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 AS builder
    Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17...
    Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17: reading manifest rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 in registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder: manifest unknown
    make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125
    + podman push image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 --tls-verify=false
    Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17: image not known
    ```
    Then operator is deployed with the unpushed image → DaemonSet never created:
    ```
    Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found
    + '[' 1781942433 -gt 1781942431 ']'
    + return 1
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `ptp-operator` Dockerfile (`FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17`) references a CI builder image that no longer exists at registry.ci.openshift.org (manifest unknown). The build pod's script uses `set +xe` / `set -x` (without restoring `-e`), so `make docker-build` and `podman push` both fail silently and the pod exits 0. The outer deploy script then runs `make deploy` pointing at an image that was never pushed; the ptp-operator pod cannot start, so it never reconciles the `PtpOperatorConfig` and the `linuxptp-daemon` DaemonSet is never created. The 400-second wait times out and the step exits 1.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — the builder image `rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was likely pruned or never promoted to registry.ci for this nightly; no source code change is responsible.
  - **Is it a flake?** No — the builder image tag is deterministically absent; every run on 4.17 (and likely 4.18 with its equivalent tag) will reproduce this until the image is re-published or the Dockerfile is updated.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Re-publish or pin to an available builder image in the `ptp-operator` Dockerfile (e.g. a quay.io mirror or a tag that is known to be retained). Additionally, restore `set -e` after the credential-hiding `set +e` block so that image build failures are not silently swallowed.
  - **Affected tests:** `e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests` (the sole test step; no individual test names extracted because deployment never completed)

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause — the missing CI builder image cascades: build fails → image not pushed → operator pod cannot start → DaemonSet not created → timeout.

**Per-Version Notes:**
- 4.17: confirmed — `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` returns `manifest unknown`
- 4.18: same root cause expected — the equivalent tag `rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18` is likely also absent; no direct evidence in this run but the failure pattern is identical

**Affected Images:** `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` (missing); `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17` (never built/pushed)

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH — the entire ptp-operator deployment is blocked on every nightly run until the builder image is restored or the Dockerfile is fixed.

99476252f94e0776.title: Timeout — context deadline exceeded
99476252f94e0776.ai_summary_short:
---

**TL;DR:** Telco lab cluster (ptpcimno) was unreachable; no PTP tests ran due to infra failure.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Telco lab cluster not provisioned / API server unreachable** (affects: all job steps post-setup)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    scp: /root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig: No such file or directory
    (cluster-setup step, 2026-06-25T05:19:38Z, exit status 1)

    ClusterVersion: Installing "" for <unknown>: <unknown>
    clusteroperators are missing

    dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout
    (gather-must-gather, gather-extra, gather-audit-logs — all fail hitting 10.8.34.117:6443)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The Ansible playbook `fetch-kubeconfig.yml` attempted to `kcli scp root@ptpcimno-installer:/root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig` but the file was absent, indicating the OCP installation on the baremetal lab cluster `ptpcimno` never completed. The API server at `10.8.34.117:6443` was unreachable for every subsequent step. The cluster-setup step exited with code 1 at 05:19:38Z — all downstream gather steps cascaded from this single infra failure. No PTP-operator code or tests ever ran.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — lab environment state issue, not a code regression.
  - **Is it a flake?** Yes — transient lab cluster provisioning failure; no code change triggered it.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Retry the job; if it recurs, inspect the `ptpcimno` lab cluster provisioning pipeline and verify the installer VM has a completed kubeconfig at `/root/ocp/auth/kubeconfig` before CI jobs are scheduled against it.
  - **Affected tests:** All steps — `e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-cluster-setup` (failed), `gather-must-gather`, `gather-extra`, `gather-audit-logs` (all cascaded failures). Zero PTP e2e tests executed.

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause — lab cluster not ready cascades into every subsequent step.

**Per-Version Notes:**
- 4.19: Confirmed — lab cluster `ptpcimno` API unreachable, cluster-setup step failed.
- 4.21: Likely same infra root cause if using the same telco lab pool; no separate evidence available.
- 4.23: Likely same infra root cause if using the same telco lab pool; no separate evidence available.

**Affected Images:** None — image imports all succeeded (`registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.19.0-0.nightly-2026-06-24-152413`, 187 images imported cleanly). No image pull failures.

**Overall Issue Class:** infra_other

**Overall Severity:** LOW

ac942a3d0e1e6ca0.title: [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]
ac942a3d0e1e6ca0.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync `GetProfileLogID`/`GetClockIDForeign` timeout):** All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit `could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded` or `timedout waiting for matches`. The test helper opens a live `kubectl logs --follow` stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

- **10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11):** After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (`Unauthorized` / `couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials`). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (`linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized`). One variant also showed `dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused`, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

- **2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite):** Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes `ts2phcState` to be forced to `PTP_FREERUN` (not `PTP_HOLDOVER`) when `NoSourceTSCount == 2` in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403`, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed `map[metric:248]` (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Pattern | Suites affected |
|---|---|
| `GetProfileLogID` / `GetClockIDForeign` log stream timeout | bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1) |
| API server Unauthorized cascade | oc (tests 2–11) |
| GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bug | tgm |

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.

No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster.** The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on `telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. **Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`0459bc0`, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes.** If the upstream sync altered the format of the `Ptp4lConf:` or `selected best master clock` log lines, the test regexes in `ptphelper.go` and `pods.go` will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. **Fix the TGM holdover state machine in `event.go:402–403`.** Remove or guard the unconditional `ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN` override when `NoSourceTSCount == 2`; instead, allow the state to transition to `PTP_HOLDOVER` so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other` (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded `test_regression` (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.


---

# Per-Suite Details


## bc

---

### Suite: bc (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to `linuxptp-daemon` pods returned `context deadline exceeded` / timeout errors during the `GetProfileLogID` step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in `GetProfileLogID`** (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

    could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
    From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod `linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg`):
    ```
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
    Additionally, the API server was returning `Unauthorized` by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:
    ```
    error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)
    E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The test helper `GetProfileLogID` in `test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go` opens a live Kubernetes log stream (`Follow: true`, no `TailLines`) via `pods.GetPodLogsRegex` searching for the regex `(?m).*?Ptp4lConf: #profile: <name>(.|\n)*?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\]` within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with `context.TODO()` so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with `context deadline exceeded` before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The `linuxptp-daemon` does emit this log line (`printNodeProfile` in `pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369` writes the rendered `Ptp4lConf` with `#profile:` prefix and `message_tag`), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive `Unauthorized` failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:**

- **2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite):** Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes `ts2phcState` to be forced to `PTP_FREERUN` (not `PTP_HOLDOVER`) when `NoSourceTSCount == 2` in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403`, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed `map[metric:248]` (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Pattern | Suites affected |
|---|---|
| `GetProfileLogID` / `GetClockIDForeign` log stream timeout | bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1) |
| API server Unauthorized cascade | oc (tests 2–11) |
| GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bug | tgm |

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.

No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster.** The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on `telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. **Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`0459bc0`, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes.** If the upstream sync altered the format of the `Ptp4lConf:` or `selected best master clock` log lines, the test regexes in `ptphelper.go` and `pods.go` will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. **Fix the TGM holdover state machine in `event.go:402–403`.** Remove or guard the unconditional `ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN` override when `NoSourceTSCount == 2`; instead, allow the state to transition to `PTP_HOLDOVER` so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other` (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded `test_regression` (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.

# Per-Suite Details


## bc

---

### Suite: bc (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to `linuxptp-daemon` pods returned `context deadline exceeded` / timeout errors during the `GetProfileLogID` step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in `GetProfileLogID`** (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

    could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
    From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod `linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg`):
    ```
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
    Additionally, the API server was returning `Unauthorized` by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:
    ```
    error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)
    E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The test helper `GetProfileLogID` in `test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go` opens a live Kubernetes log stream (`Follow: true`, no `TailLines`) via `pods.GetPodLogsRegex` searching for the regex `(?m).*?Ptp4lConf: #profile: <name>(.|\n)*?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\]` within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with `context.TODO()` so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with `context deadline exceeded` before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The `linuxptp-daemon` does emit this log line (`printNodeProfile` in `pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369` writes the rendered `Ptp4lConf` with `#profile:` prefix and `message_tag`), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive `Unauthorized` failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:**

    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
    Additionally, the API server was returning `Unauthorized` by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:
    ```
    error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)
    E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The test helper `GetProfileLogID` in `test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go` opens a live Kubernetes log stream (`Follow: true`, no `TailLines`) via `pods.GetPodLogsRegex` searching for the regex `(?m).*?Ptp4lConf: #profile: <name>(.|\n)*?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\]` within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with `context.TODO()` so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with `context deadline exceeded` before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The `linuxptp-daemon` does emit this log line (`printNodeProfile` in `pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369` writes the rendered `Ptp4lConf` with `#profile:` prefix and `message_tag`), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive `Unauthorized` failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:**

ea783aea8b4de425.title: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]
ea783aea8b4de425.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync `GetProfileLogID`/`GetClockIDForeign` timeout):** All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit `could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded` or `timedout waiting for matches`. The test helper opens a live `kubectl logs --follow` stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

- **10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11):** After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (`Unauthorized` / `couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials`). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (`linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized`). One variant also showed `dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused`, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

- **2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite):** Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes `ts2phcState` to be forced to `PTP_FREERUN` (not `PTP_HOLDOVER`) when `NoSourceTSCount == 2` in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403`, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed `map[metric:248]` (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Pattern | Suites affected |
|---|---|
| `GetProfileLogID` / `GetClockIDForeign` log stream timeout | bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1) |
| API server Unauthorized cascade | oc (tests 2–11) |
| GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bug | tgm |

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.

No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster.** The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on `telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. **Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`0459bc0`, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes.** If the upstream sync altered the format of the `Ptp4lConf:` or `selected best master clock` log lines, the test regexes in `ptphelper.go` and `pods.go` will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. **Fix the TGM holdover state machine in `event.go:402–403`.** Remove or guard the unconditional `ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN` override when `NoSourceTSCount == 2`; instead, allow the state to transition to `PTP_HOLDOVER` so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other` (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded `test_regression` (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.


---

|---|---|

1. **Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster.** The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on `telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). **Suite TL;DR:** All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to `linuxptp-daemon` pods returned `context deadline exceeded` / timeout errors during the `GetProfileLogID` step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.

  - **Evidence:**

    could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
    From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod `linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg`):
    ```
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

    Additionally, the API server was returning `Unauthorized` by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:
    ```
    error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)
    E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The test helper `GetProfileLogID` in `test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go` opens a live Kubernetes log stream (`Follow: true`, no `TailLines`) via `pods.GetPodLogsRegex` searching for the regex `(?m).*?Ptp4lConf: #profile: <name>(.|\n)*?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\]` within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with `context.TODO()` so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with `context deadline exceeded` before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The `linuxptp-daemon` does emit this log line (`printNodeProfile` in `pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369` writes the rendered `Ptp4lConf` with `#profile:` prefix and `message_tag`), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive `Unauthorized` failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:**

e7a58227205e5246.title: [It] [oc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]
e7a58227205e5246.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync `GetProfileLogID`/`GetClockIDForeign` timeout):** All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit `could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded` or `timedout waiting for matches`. The test helper opens a live `kubectl logs --follow` stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

- **10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11):** After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (`Unauthorized` / `couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials`). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (`linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized`). One variant also showed `dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused`, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

- **2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite):** Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes `ts2phcState` to be forced to `PTP_FREERUN` (not `PTP_HOLDOVER`) when `NoSourceTSCount == 2` in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403`, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed `map[metric:248]` (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Pattern | Suites affected |
|---|---|
| `GetProfileLogID` / `GetClockIDForeign` log stream timeout | bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1) |
| API server Unauthorized cascade | oc (tests 2–11) |
| GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bug | tgm |

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.

No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster.** The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on `telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. **Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`0459bc0`, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes.** If the upstream sync altered the format of the `Ptp4lConf:` or `selected best master clock` log lines, the test regexes in `ptphelper.go` and `pods.go` will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. **Fix the TGM holdover state machine in `event.go:402–403`.** Remove or guard the unconditional `ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN` override when `NoSourceTSCount == 2`; instead, allow the state to transition to `PTP_HOLDOVER` so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other` (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded `test_regression` (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.


---

# Per-Suite Details


## bc

### Suite: bc (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to `linuxptp-daemon` pods returned `context deadline exceeded` / timeout errors during the `GetProfileLogID` step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in `GetProfileLogID`** (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

    could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
    From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod `linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg`):
    ```
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...
    [ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
    Additionally, the API server was returning `Unauthorized` by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:
    ```

The stream — opened with `context.TODO()` so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with `context deadline exceeded` before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The `linuxptp-daemon` does emit this log line (`printNodeProfile` in `pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369` writes the rendered `Ptp4lConf` with `#profile:` prefix and `message_tag`), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test.   - **Breaking PR/Commit:**

**TL;DR:** Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync `GetProfileLogID`/`GetClockIDForeign` timeout):** All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit `could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded` or `timedout waiting for matches`. The test helper opens a live `kubectl logs --follow` stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

- **10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11):** After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (`Unauthorized` / `couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials`). - **2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite):** Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes `ts2phcState` to be forced to `PTP_FREERUN` (not `PTP_HOLDOVER`) when `NoSourceTSCount == 2` in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403`, making ClockClass 7 unreachable.   - **Root Cause:** The test helper `GetProfileLogID` in `test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go` opens a live Kubernetes log stream (`Follow: true`, no `TailLines`) via `pods.GetPodLogsRegex` searching for the regex `(?m).*?Ptp4lConf: #profile: <name>(.|\n)*?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\]` within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with `context.TODO()` so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with `context deadline exceeded` before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The `linuxptp-daemon` does emit this log line (`printNodeProfile` in `pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369` writes the rendered `Ptp4lConf` with `#profile:` prefix and `message_tag`), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive `Unauthorized` failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:**

5f65a4dc4ee981a2.title: [It] [oc-serial] PTP e2e tests Running with event enabled Event API version validation Should default to event API v2 when apiVersion is not explicitly set in PtpOperatorConfig [Serial]
5f65a4dc4ee981a2.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync `GetProfileLogID`/`GetClockIDForeign` timeout):** All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit `could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded` or `timedout waiting for matches`. The test helper opens a live `kubectl logs --follow` stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

- **10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11):** After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (`Unauthorized` / `couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials`). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (`linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized`). One variant also showed `dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused`, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

- **2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite):** Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes `ts2phcState` to be forced to `PTP_FREERUN` (not `PTP_HOLDOVER`) when `NoSourceTSCount == 2` in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403`, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed `map[metric:248]` (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Pattern | Suites affected |
|---|---|
| `GetProfileLogID` / `GetClockIDForeign` log stream timeout | bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1) |
| API server Unauthorized cascade | oc (tests 2–11) |
| GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bug | tgm |

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.

No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster.** The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on `telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. **Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`0459bc0`, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes.** If the upstream sync altered the format of the `Ptp4lConf:` or `selected best master clock` log lines, the test regexes in `ptphelper.go` and `pods.go` will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. **Fix the TGM holdover state machine in `event.go:402–403`.** Remove or guard the unconditional `ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN` override when `NoSourceTSCount == 2`; instead, allow the state to transition to `PTP_HOLDOVER` so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `infra_other` (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded `test_regression` (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.


---

ba422e8f8af0f424.title: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
ba422e8f8af0f424.ai_summary_short:
---

**TL;DR:** WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 DPLL never entered HOLDOVER (clock class 7) after GNSS coldboot.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: DPLL fails to reach/maintain clock class 7 (HOLDOVER) after GNSS loss** (affects Tests: both)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # Test 1 — DPLL stuck at FREERUN for full 200s after GNSS disabled:
    Matched line: [ptp4l 248]  → GNSS disabled → Clock class not yet 7, retrying... (×32+)
    → GNSS enabled → [FAILED] Timed out waiting for clock class 7 (ptp.go:3802, 3m20s elapsed)

    # Test 2 — class 7 transient, immediately overtaken by 248:
    PtpClockClassChange Event recieved  map[metric:6], map[metric:7], map[metric:248]
    GnssStateChange Event recieved  map[metric:0 notification:FAILURE-NOFIX]
    → STEP: Verifying ClockClass value to 7 → [FAILED] expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248] (ptp.go:3898)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** After GNSS signal loss on node `cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com`, the WPC hardware DPLL transitions directly from LOCKED to FREERUN (class 248) without sustaining HOLDOVER (class 7). In `pkg/event/event.go`, clock class 7 requires `dpllState == PTP_HOLDOVER`; since the DPLL reports FREERUN immediately, that path is never taken. In test 2, HOLDOVER was briefly entered (class 7 visible in the event stream) but exhausted faster than the metric-polling step could verify it — confirming the DPLL holdover window on this hardware is abnormally short.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — the upstream sync PR #613 (`0459bc0`, CNF-24996/OCPBUGS-90708, merged 2026-06-23) was an upstream sync to the main branch and does not directly affect the `release-4.22` branch under test; no code regression identified.
  - **Is it a flake?** Yes — test 2 shows holdover *does* occur but transiently; this is a hardware-timing sensitivity, consistent with intermittent failures when DPLL holdover duration is at the margins.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Investigate DPLL holdover configuration on `cnfdf32` (check `LocalHoldoverTimeout` configured for the WPC hardware); if hardware is degraded or misconfigured for holdover, reprovisioning or reconfiguring the node should resolve it. Retry the job to confirm transience.
  - **Affected tests:**
    - `[It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]`
    - `[It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]`

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single group — both failures share the same root cause: DPLL on cnfdf32 not sustaining HOLDOVER state long enough after GNSS loss. 12be34d0791f8f7c.title: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]
12be34d0791f8f7c.ai_summary_short:
---

**TL;DR:** WPC T-GM node cnfdf32 DPLL never entered HOLDOVER (clock class 7) after GNSS coldboot.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: DPLL fails to reach/maintain clock class 7 (HOLDOVER) after GNSS loss** (affects Tests: both)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # Test 1 — DPLL stuck at FREERUN for full 200s after GNSS disabled:
    Matched line: [ptp4l 248]  → GNSS disabled → Clock class not yet 7, retrying... (×32+)
    → GNSS enabled → [FAILED] Timed out waiting for clock class 7 (ptp.go:3802, 3m20s elapsed)

    # Test 2 — class 7 transient, immediately overtaken by 248:
    PtpClockClassChange Event recieved  map[metric:6], map[metric:7], map[metric:248]
    GnssStateChange Event recieved  map[metric:0 notification:FAILURE-NOFIX]
    → STEP: Verifying ClockClass value to 7 → [FAILED] expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248] (ptp.go:3898)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** After GNSS signal loss on node `cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com`, the WPC hardware DPLL transitions directly from LOCKED to FREERUN (class 248) without sustaining HOLDOVER (class 7). In `pkg/event/event.go`, clock class 7 requires `dpllState == PTP_HOLDOVER`; since the DPLL reports FREERUN immediately, that path is never taken. In test 2, HOLDOVER was briefly entered (class 7 visible in the event stream) but exhausted faster than the metric-polling step could verify it — confirming the DPLL holdover window on this hardware is abnormally short.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — the upstream sync PR #613 (`0459bc0`, CNF-24996/OCPBUGS-90708, merged 2026-06-23) was an upstream sync to the main branch and does not directly affect the `release-4.22` branch under test; no code regression identified.
  - **Is it a flake?** Yes — test 2 shows holdover *does* occur but transiently; this is a hardware-timing sensitivity, consistent with intermittent failures when DPLL holdover duration is at the margins.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Investigate DPLL holdover configuration on `cnfdf32` (check `LocalHoldoverTimeout` configured for the WPC hardware); if hardware is degraded or misconfigured for holdover, reprovisioning or reconfiguring the node should resolve it. Retry the job to confirm transience.
  - **Affected tests:**
    - `[It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]`
    - `[It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]`

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single group — both failures share the same root cause: DPLL on cnfdf32 not sustaining HOLDOVER state long enough after GNSS loss. Test 1 never sees class 7 at all; test 2 sees it transiently. Both are direct manifestations of the same abnormally short holdover window on this hardware.

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 4.22 (nightly-2026-06-23-164229) — both failures in the `[tgm-serial]` suite only; all other suites (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc) show 0 failures and passed cleanly.

**Affected Images:** `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.22`, `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon:4.22`, `image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/cloud-event-proxy:4.22`

**Overall Issue Class:** test_flake

**Overall Severity:** LOW

af71962cadde906c.title: [It] [dualfollower-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]
af71962cadde906c.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests**
  Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting `linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8` never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit `context deadline exceeded` after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on `Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM` / `<bool>: false`. The dualfollower node `cnfdf31` exhibits both missing `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` and `ens2f3np3` stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.

- **5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` rejected as two bad commands**
  Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). `pmc` is called with `GET` and `PARENT_DATA_SET` as separate argv elements; the binary responds `bad command: GET` / `bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET`. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. `GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET`) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Root Cause | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GM FREERUN / GNSS never locked | All 3 | ~6 | ~6 | All 5 |
| pmc split-arg bug | — | 3 | 2 | — |

- The GM FREERUN failure is **shared across all 4 suites** — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
- The pmc split-arg bug is **shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only** (suites that exercise `PARENT_DATA_SET` queries via the BC node).
- No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first.** Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and `ts2phc` DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. **Fix pmc invocation to pass `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` as a single quoted argument.** In both the test helper (`ptp-operator-conformance-test`) and any `exec.Command` / shell call in `linuxptp-daemon`, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. `exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")`), or switch to the interactive `RunPMCExpGetParentDS` path with lowercase command (`"get PARENT_DATA_SET"`) to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. **Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job.** Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_flake` (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + `test_regression` (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync `0459bc0`)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.


---

# Per-Suite Details


## dualfollower

---

### Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node `cnfdf31`'s follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and `ens2f3np3` was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING** (affects: all 3 tests)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # Tests 1 & 2 — identical error
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,
        available op[tions: ...]
    ```
    ```
    # Test 3 — additional symptom
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="<nil>",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found
    ```
    ```
    # Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)
    parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:
      bad command: GET
      bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET
    ```
    ```
    # tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)
    Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)
    ...clock class: 248  (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric (registered in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63` with labels `{process, node, iface}`) is only set when `UpdateClockStateMetrics()` in `pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23` is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination `{node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0}` being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on `cnfdf31` never processed a servo state log line for `ens2f0np0` — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit `role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)` in Test 3 for `ens2f3np3` corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the `pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET` errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, `linuxptp-daemon` branch `release-5.0`) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit `OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026)` in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit `OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs` in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996`, merged 2026-06-23, SHA `0459bc0`) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (`OCPBUGS-88369`, merged 2026-06-25, SHA `fc5d567`) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
  - **Is it a flake?** No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa
