105eef9d7450d996.title: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]
105eef9d7450d996.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests**
  Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting `linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8` never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit `context deadline exceeded` after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on `Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM` / `<bool>: false`. The dualfollower node `cnfdf31` exhibits both missing `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` and `ens2f3np3` stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.

- **5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` rejected as two bad commands**
  Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). `pmc` is called with `GET` and `PARENT_DATA_SET` as separate argv elements; the binary responds `bad command: GET` / `bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET`. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. `GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET`) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Root Cause | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GM FREERUN / GNSS never locked | All 3 | ~6 | ~6 | All 5 |
| pmc split-arg bug | — | 3 | 2 | — |

- The GM FREERUN failure is **shared across all 4 suites** — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
- The pmc split-arg bug is **shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only** (suites that exercise `PARENT_DATA_SET` queries via the BC node).
- No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first.** Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and `ts2phc` DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. **Fix pmc invocation to pass `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` as a single quoted argument.** In both the test helper (`ptp-operator-conformance-test`) and any `exec.Command` / shell call in `linuxptp-daemon`, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. `exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")`), or switch to the interactive `RunPMCExpGetParentDS` path with lowercase command (`"get PARENT_DATA_SET"`) to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. **Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job.** Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_flake` (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + `test_regression` (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync `0459bc0`)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

95bc04f7ae5b7162.title: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock [Serial]
95bc04f7ae5b7162.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests**
  Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting `linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8` never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit `context deadline exceeded` after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on `Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM` / `<bool>: false`. The dualfollower node `cnfdf31` exhibits both missing `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` and `ens2f3np3` stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.

- **5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` rejected as two bad commands**
  Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). `pmc` is called with `GET` and `PARENT_DATA_SET` as separate argv elements; the binary responds `bad command: GET` / `bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET`. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. `GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET`) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Root Cause | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GM FREERUN / GNSS never locked | All 3 | ~6 | ~6 | All 5 |
| pmc split-arg bug | — | 3 | 2 | — |

- The GM FREERUN failure is **shared across all 4 suites** — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
- The pmc split-arg bug is **shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only** (suites that exercise `PARENT_DATA_SET` queries via the BC node).
- No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first.** Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and `ts2phc` DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. **Fix pmc invocation to pass `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` as a single quoted argument.** In both the test helper (`ptp-operator-conformance-test`) and any `exec.Command` / shell call in `linuxptp-daemon`, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. `exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")`), or switch to the interactive `RunPMCExpGetParentDS` path with lowercase command (`"get PARENT_DATA_SET"`) to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. **Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job.** Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_flake` (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + `test_regression` (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync `0459bc0`)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.


---

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node `cnfdf31`'s follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and `ens2f3np3` was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.

    ```
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,
        available op[tions: ...]
    ```
    ```

    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="<nil>",

    ```
    ```

    ```

The commit `OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026)` in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit `OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs` in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996`, merged 2026-06-23, SHA `0459bc0`) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (`OCPBUGS-88369`, merged 2026-06-25, SHA `fc5d567`) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. 9d5d8c8a67edd345.title: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP Outage recovery The slave node network interface is taken down and up [Serial]
9d5d8c8a67edd345.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests**
  Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting `linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8` never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit `context deadline exceeded` after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on `Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM` / `<bool>: false`. The dualfollower node `cnfdf31` exhibits both missing `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` and `ens2f3np3` stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.

- **5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` rejected as two bad commands**
  Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). `pmc` is called with `GET` and `PARENT_DATA_SET` as separate argv elements; the binary responds `bad command: GET` / `bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET`. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. `GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET`) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Root Cause | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GM FREERUN / GNSS never locked | All 3 | ~6 | ~6 | All 5 |
| pmc split-arg bug | — | 3 | 2 | — |

- The GM FREERUN failure is **shared across all 4 suites** — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
- The pmc split-arg bug is **shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only** (suites that exercise `PARENT_DATA_SET` queries via the BC node).
- No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first.** Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and `ts2phc` DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. **Fix pmc invocation to pass `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` as a single quoted argument.** In both the test helper (`ptp-operator-conformance-test`) and any `exec.Command` / shell call in `linuxptp-daemon`, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. `exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")`), or switch to the interactive `RunPMCExpGetParentDS` path with lowercase command (`"get PARENT_DATA_SET"`) to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. **Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job.** Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_flake` (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + `test_regression` (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync `0459bc0`)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.


---

# Per-Suite Details


## dualfollower

---

### Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node `cnfdf31`'s follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and `ens2f3np3` was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING** (affects: all 3 tests)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # Tests 1 & 2 — identical error
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,
        available op[tions: ...]
    ```
    ```
    # Test 3 — additional symptom
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="<nil>",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found
    ```
    ```
    # Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)
    parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:
      bad command: GET
      bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET
    ```
    ```
    # tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)
    Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)
    ...clock class: 248  (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric (registered in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63` with labels `{process, node, iface}`) is only set when `UpdateClockStateMetrics()` in `pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23` is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination `{node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0}` being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on `cnfdf31` never processed a servo state log line for `ens2f0np0` — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit `role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)` in Test 3 for `ens2f3np3` corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the `pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET` errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, `linuxptp-daemon` branch `release-5.0`) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit `OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026)` in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit `OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs` in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996`, merged 2026-06-23, SHA `0459bc0`) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (`OCPBUGS-88369`, merged 2026-06-25, SHA `fc5d567`) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
  - **Is it a flake?** No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa

- **20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests**
  Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting `linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8` never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit `context deadline exceeded` after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on `Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM` / `<bool>: false`. The dualfollower node `cnfdf31` exhibits both missing `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` and `ens2f3np3` stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.

- **5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` rejected as two bad commands**
  Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). `pmc` is called with `GET` and `PARENT_DATA_SET` as separate argv elements; the binary responds `bad command: GET` / `bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET`. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. `GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET`) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Root Cause | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GM FREERUN / GNSS never locked | All 3 | ~6 | ~6 | All 5 |
| pmc split-arg bug | — | 3 | 2 | — |

- The GM FREERUN failure is **shared across all 4 suites** — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
- The pmc split-arg bug is **shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only** (suites that exercise `PARENT_DATA_SET` queries via the BC node).
- No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first.** Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and `ts2phc` DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. **Fix pmc invocation to pass `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` as a single quoted argument.** In both the test helper (`ptp-operator-conformance-test`) and any `exec.Command` / shell call in `linuxptp-daemon`, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. `exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")`), or switch to the interactive `RunPMCExpGetParentDS` path with lowercase command (`"get PARENT_DATA_SET"`) to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. **Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job.** Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_flake` (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + `test_regression` (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync `0459bc0`)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.


---

# Per-Suite Details


## dualfollower

---

### Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node `cnfdf31`'s follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and `ens2f3np3` was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING** (affects: all 3 tests)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # Tests 1 & 2 — identical error
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,
        available op[tions: ...]
    ```
    ```
    # Test 3 — additional symptom
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="<nil>",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found
    ```
    ```
    # Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)
    parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:
      bad command: GET
      bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET
    ```
    ```
    # tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)
    Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)
    ...clock class: 248  (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric (registered in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63` with labels `{process, node, iface}`) is only set when `UpdateClockStateMetrics()` in `pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23` is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination `{node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0}` being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on `cnfdf31` never processed a servo state log line for `ens2f0np0` — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit `role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)` in Test 3 for `ens2f3np3` corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the `pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET` errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, `linuxptp-daemon` branch `release-5.0`) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit `OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026)` in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit `OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs` in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996`, merged 2026-06-23, SHA `0459bc0`) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (`OCPBUGS-88369`, merged 2026-06-25, SHA `fc5d567`) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
  - **Is it a flake?** No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa

Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. `GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET`) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Root Cause | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GM FREERUN / GNSS never locked | All 3 | ~6 | ~6 | All 5 |
| pmc split-arg bug | — | 3 | 2 | — |

- The GM FREERUN failure is **shared across all 4 suites** — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
- The pmc split-arg bug is **shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only** (suites that exercise `PARENT_DATA_SET` queries via the BC node).
- No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first.** Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and `ts2phc` DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. **Fix pmc invocation to pass `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` as a single quoted argument.** In both the test helper (`ptp-operator-conformance-test`) and any `exec.Command` / shell call in `linuxptp-daemon`, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. `exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")`), or switch to the interactive `RunPMCExpGetParentDS` path with lowercase command (`"get PARENT_DATA_SET"`) to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. **Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job.** Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_flake` (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + `test_regression` (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync `0459bc0`)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

## dualfollower

---

### Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node `cnfdf31`'s follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and `ens2f3np3` was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING** (affects: all 3 tests)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # Tests 1 & 2 — identical error
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,
        available op[tions: ...]
    ```
    ```
    # Test 3 — additional symptom
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="<nil>",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found
    ```
    ```

      bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET
    ```

The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
  - **Is it a flake?** No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa

1. **Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first.** Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and `ts2phc` DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. **Fix pmc invocation to pass `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` as a single quoted argument.** In both the test helper (`ptp-operator-conformance-test`) and any `exec.Command` / shell call in `linuxptp-daemon`, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. `exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")`), or switch to the interactive `RunPMCExpGetParentDS` path with lowercase command (`"get PARENT_DATA_SET"`) to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. **Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job.** Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. **Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

# Per-Suite Details


## dualfollower

---

### Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node `cnfdf31`'s follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and `ens2f3np3` was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING** (affects: all 3 tests)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # Tests 1 & 2 — identical error
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,
        available op[tions: ...]
    ```
    ```
    # Test 3 — additional symptom
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="<nil>",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found
    ```
    ```
    # Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)
    parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:
      bad command: GET
      bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET
    ```
    ```
    # tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

    ...clock class: 248  (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric (registered in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63` with labels `{process, node, iface}`) is only set when `UpdateClockStateMetrics()` in `pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23` is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination `{node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0}` being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on `cnfdf31` never processed a servo state log line for `ens2f0np0` — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run.   - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (`OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996`, merged 2026-06-23, SHA `0459bc0`) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (`OCPBUGS-88369`, merged 2026-06-25, SHA `fc5d567`) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
  - **Is it a flake?** No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa

e257c59bf09dc184.title: [It] [dualnicbcha-parallel] Event based tests PTP Slave Clock Sync
e257c59bf09dc184.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests**
  Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting `linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8` never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit `context deadline exceeded` after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on `Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM` / `<bool>: false`. The dualfollower node `cnfdf31` exhibits both missing `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` and `ens2f3np3` stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.

- **5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` rejected as two bad commands**
  Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). `pmc` is called with `GET` and `PARENT_DATA_SET` as separate argv elements; the binary responds `bad command: GET` / `bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET`. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. `GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET`) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

|---|---|---|---|---|

**Suite TL;DR:** All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node `cnfdf31`'s follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and `ens2f3np3` was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING** (affects: all 3 tests)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # Tests 1 & 2 — identical error
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,
        available op[tions: ...]
    ```
    ```
    # Test 3 — additional symptom
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="<nil>",
     slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found
    ```

The explicit `role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)` in Test 3 for `ens2f3np3` corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. 069b9cea01a4da88.title: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]
069b9cea01a4da88.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests**
  Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting `linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8` never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit `context deadline exceeded` after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on `Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM` / `<bool>: false`. The dualfollower node `cnfdf31` exhibits both missing `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric for `ens2f0np0` and `ens2f3np3` stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.

- **5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` rejected as two bad commands**
  Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). `pmc` is called with `GET` and `PARENT_DATA_SET` as separate argv elements; the binary responds `bad command: GET` / `bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET`. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. `GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET`) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Root Cause | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GM FREERUN / GNSS never locked | All 3 | ~6 | ~6 | All 5 |
| pmc split-arg bug | — | 3 | 2 | — |

- The GM FREERUN failure is **shared across all 4 suites** — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
- The pmc split-arg bug is **shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only** (suites that exercise `PARENT_DATA_SET` queries via the BC node).
- No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first.** Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and `ts2phc` DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. **Fix pmc invocation to pass `GET PARENT_DATA_SET` as a single quoted argument.** In both the test helper (`ptp-operator-conformance-test`) and any `exec.Command` / shell call in `linuxptp-daemon`, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. `exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")`), or switch to the interactive `RunPMCExpGetParentDS` path with lowercase command (`"get PARENT_DATA_SET"`) to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. **Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job.** Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_flake` (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + `test_regression` (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync `0459bc0`)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

    ```
      ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
        nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found
    ```
    ```
    # Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)
    parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:
      bad command: GET
      bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET
    ```
    ```
    # tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)
    Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)
    ...clock class: 248  (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The `openshift_ptp_clock_state` metric (registered in `linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63` with labels `{process, node, iface}`) is only set when `UpdateClockStateMetrics()` in `pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23` is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination `{node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0}` being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on `cnfdf31` never processed a servo state log line for `ens2f0np0` — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit `role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)` in Test 3 for `ens2f3np3` corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the `pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET` errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, `linuxptp-daemon` branch `release-5.0`) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit `OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026)` in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit `OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs` in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.

The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
  - **Is it a flake?** No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa

0ee2995536491c6a.title: [It] [dualnicbcha-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails [Serial]
0ee2995536491c6a.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** T-GM never achieved GNSS lock (FREERUN/clockClass=248), cascading to all 25 BC/follower sync failures job-wide.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **20/25 failures — T-GM stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248, never GNSS-locked):** The grandmaster node (`linuxptp-daemon-pljkt`, `cnfdf30`) never achieved clock class 6, starving every downstream node of a valid PTP source. This is the primary root cause, confirmed across all four suites: tgm (5/5), dualnicbc (3/9 directly, 3/9 indirectly via BC never locking), dualnicbcha (5/8 directly via BC FREERUN), and dualfollower (3/3 — follower metrics never populated). The linuxptp RPM pulled from CentOS Stream 9 at image build time likely introduced a ptp4l/ts2phc interface regression preventing GNSS-lock propagation.

- **5/25 failures — `pmc` argument-split bug in `ci/telco-runner:latest` image:** Three dualnicbc tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and two dualnicbcha tests ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master") fail with `bad command: GET` / `bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET` — the pmc management command was split into two separate argv tokens in the pre-built image, whereas the current source passes them as a single string. This is a deterministic, non-flaky regression in the `ci/telco-runner:latest` image.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Root Cause | tgm | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | dualfollower |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| T-GM FREERUN (clock class 248) | 5/5 (primary) | 6/9 (GM & BC) | 3/8 (BC via GM) | 3/3 (follower metrics absent) |
| pmc argv-split bug | — | 3/9 | 2/8 | — |

All four suites share the T-GM FREERUN root cause — it is job-wide, not suite-specific. The pmc argv-split bug is confined to dualnicbc and dualnicbcha (suites that use pmc-based clock-ID or parent-data-set queries). The tgm and dualfollower suites do not invoke pmc directly and are unaffected by it.

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Fix the T-GM GNSS lock (highest priority, unblocks 20/25 failures):** Pin the `linuxptp` RPM version in the Dockerfile (or lock to a known-good snapshot repo) instead of pulling the latest CentOS Stream 9 package at build time. Verify that ts2phc is correctly receiving GNSS signal on `cnfdf30` and that the ptp4l/ts2phc IPC interface is intact for the pinned version. Run `yum downgrade linuxptp` to the last known-good version as an immediate mitigation.

2. **Rebuild `ci/telco-runner:latest` from current `main` (fixes 5/25 pmc failures):** The current ptp-operator `main` branch has already replaced the broken pmc-based `getForeignClockIDViaPMC` path with log-based detection (`ptphelper.GetClockIDForeign`), eliminating the argv-split bug entirely. The telco-runner image just needs to be rebuilt from HEAD.

3. **Pin image build timestamps or use digest-locked base images:** Both root causes originate from unpinned external package versions (`yum -y update` + `yum -y install linuxptp` at build time). Introduce digest pinning or a locked RPM repo to make builds reproducible and prevent silent regressions from upstream package updates.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_regression` (the infrastructure/hardware environment appears functional; both failures trace to software regressions — a linuxptp RPM version change and a stale pre-built image)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL (20/25 failures cascade from a single unresolved T-GM lock failure; the entire PTP synchronization chain is broken job-wide)


---

# Per-Suite Details


## dualfollower

---

### Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All three dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out because the follower node `cnfdf31` never achieved a LOCKED state on either slave interface — traced upstream to the T-GM itself never locking to GNSS (clock class stuck at 248 / FREERUN).

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: T-GM not locked → follower clock state metrics never populated** (affects: all 3 tests)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    # From junit-failures.txt — Tests 1 & 3:
    no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1
    (node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",
    slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
      nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,
      available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

    # From junit-failures.txt — Test 2:
      ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)
      ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,
      nodeName: cnfdf31...

    # From junit-failures.txt — upstream cause (tgm suite):
    Test: [tgm-serial] WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on metrics
    Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM
    Expected <bool>: false to be true

    Test: [tgm-serial] WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on logs
    could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-pljkt
    container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The T-GM node never achieved clock class 6 (LOCKED), remaining at class 248 (FREERUN/default) for the entire run, as shown by the tgm-serial suite failures. Without a locked grandmaster advertising a valid PTP clock, the follower interfaces on `cnfdf31` have no master to sync from: `ptp4l` on `ens2f0np0` never reaches the `s2` servo state that would trigger `updateClockStateMetrics()` (`pkg/daemon/metrics.go:~line 299`) to emit the `openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"}` Prometheus metric — so the test's metric lookup returns "not found". `ens2f3np3` is left in `LISTENING` (it received no Announce from a master, never promoted to `SLAVE`). The `register.go` in `pkg/metrics` registers `ClockState` as a `GaugeVec` with `{process, node, iface}` labels; the vector entry for `ens2f0np0` is simply never written.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — the test ran on 2026-06-21 against code built from the `main` branches of `linuxptp-daemon`, `cloud-event-proxy`, and `ptp-operator`. The next recorded commit to `linuxptp-daemon` is `0459bc0` (merged 2026-06-23, "CNF-24996, OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream 22-Jun-2026"), which post-dates the run and is therefore not the direct cause. Check commits to `linuxptp-daemon` and `ptp-operator` main merged on or before 2026-06-20/21 that touch ts2phc, GNSS driver, or GM locking logic.
  - **Is it a flake?** Possibly — a transient GNSS signal loss at the lab would produce this exact pattern across all suites. However the simultaneous `pmc bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET` failures in the dualnicbc/dualnicbcha suites point toward a software regression (pmc protocol rejection by ptp4l) rather than pure infra. Treat as **not a confirmed flake** until the job is retried and the tgm lock state is verified.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Rerun the job immediately; if the T-GM still fails to lock, bisect recent `linuxptp-daemon` and `ptp-operator` commits touching ts2phc GNSS acquisition or ptp4l management interface (the `pmc` "bad command" in other suites is a correlated signal). If the GM locks on retry, file as an infra flake (GNSS intermittency) and monitor frequency.

**Suite Issue Class:** infra_other (upstream GM never locked; may also involve a software regression)
**Suite Severity:** HIGH

## dualnicbc

---

### Suite: dualnicbc (9 failures)

**Suite TL;DR:** All 9 failures stem from two independent causes: ptp4l on the BC never reaching locked state (clock class stuck at 248/FREERUN rather than 6/LOCKED), and a pmc command invocation incompatibility in the ci-telco-runner test binary causing "bad command" responses.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: pmc non-interactive command format incompatibility** (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downst

ce6143779aae984d.title: No meaningful output found in logs
ce6143779aae984d.ai_summary_short:
I now have all the data needed. 

---

**TL;DR:** Worker node `cnfdf30` was `NotReady/Unknown` at test start; pre-flight check aborted before any PTP tests ran.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Unhealthy cluster node prevented test execution** (affects Tests: `e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream-telco5g-ptp-tests`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com   NotReady,SchedulingDisabled   worker   15m
    NOT_READY_NODES='cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com Unknown'
    [ERROR] All nodes must be Ready before starting PTP tests. Aborting.
    + exit 1
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** The test step's pre-flight script (`telco5g-ptp-tests`) checks that all cluster nodes are `Ready` before launching PTP tests. Worker node `cnfdf30.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com` was in `NotReady,SchedulingDisabled` with condition `Unknown` at the time the test pod started. The script detected this via `oc get nodes` and exited 1 immediately — no PTP tests ran at all. The node being in `SchedulingDisabled/Unknown` indicates it was either mid-reboot, cordoned by a prior step, or lost contact with the control plane.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown — this is a transient infrastructure/cluster state issue.
  - **Is it a flake?** Yes — node `NotReady/Unknown` at test start time is a classic transient infra flake caused by hardware or network instability in the bare-metal telco5g pool.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Re-run the job; if the node is consistently unhealthy, investigate the bare-metal host `cnfdf30` in the telco5g pool and re-provision or replace it. Optionally extend the pre-flight check to wait/retry for node readiness instead of immediately aborting.
  - **Affected tests:** All PTP tests (none ran; job aborted at pre-flight)

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause — one bad cluster node. No code regression involved.

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 4.22 (nightly 2026-06-07-152904) — cluster infrastructure issue on bare-metal telco5g pool; unrelated to the OCP version itself.

**Affected Images:** `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.22.0-0.nightly-2026-06-07-152904` (successfully imported; not the cause)

**Overall Issue Class:** infra_other

**Overall Severity:** MEDIUM

bf0d8f3f95fa7196.title: Matrix mismatch: 6 port(s) in matrix but not in use; 8 port(s) open but missing endpointslice
bf0d8f3f95fa7196.ai_summary_short:
Now I have a complete picture. 

---

**TL;DR:** OCP 4.23 SNO crio ephemeral port (TCP 36039) escapes filtering after PR #400 removed Linux dynamic port range.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: CRI-O ephemeral port open without EndpointSlice (causes test failure)**
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    [FAIL] Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports
    /tmp/secret/commatrix/test/e2e/validation_test.go:161
    LISTEN 0   4096   10.0.16.43:36039 0.0.0.0:* users:(("crio",pid=2681,fd=11))
    - Ingress,TCP,36039,,crio,,,master,false  (in matrix-diff-ss; no EndpointSlice)
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** CRI-O binds an ephemeral TCP port (36039, in Linux range 32768-60999) on the node IP. This port has no Kubernetes EndpointSlice. In `validation_test.go:157-161`, `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` only skips `rpc.statd`/`dnsmasq`; `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges` (line 159) consults `commatrix.DynamicRanges`, which after commit `7d56343` ("Remove Linux Dynamic ranges from generated communication matrix", PR #400) no longer includes the 32768-60999 ephemeral range — only the NodePort range 30000-32767. TCP 36039 therefore survives both filters and triggers `Fail()` at line 161. The ovnkube nodeport ports (30146/30767/32633) and rpc.statd ports are correctly filtered, leaving only TCP 36039 as the trigger.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** `7d56343` / PR #400 (`1fec55d Merge pull request #400 from shirmoran/remove-linux-dynamic-range`) — removed the Linux ephemeral range from the generated commatrix and thus from `DynamicRanges`.
  - **Is it a flake?** No — this is a regression; the specific ephemeral port number varies per boot but the root cause (missing ephemeral range from DynamicRanges) is deterministic and will reproduce on every run.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Either re-add crio (and similar host-level ephemeral services) to the `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` allowlist in `validation_test.go`, or re-introduce the Linux ephemeral range (32768-60999) as a filtered dynamic range separate from the generated commatrix documentation.
  - **Affected tests:** `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports` (validation_test.go:161)

- **Group 2: Stale entries in documented matrix for OCP 4.23 SNO (warnings, not direct failure)**
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    doc-diff-commatrix:
    - Ingress,TCP,80,openshift-ingress,router-default,...
    - Ingress,TCP,443,openshift-ingress,router-default,...
    - Ingress,TCP,8798,openshift-machine-config-operator,...
    - Ingress,TCP,9192,openshift-cluster-machine-approver,...
    matrix-diff-ss:
    + Ingress,TCP,9443,openshift-cloud-controller-manager-operator,...
    + Ingress,TCP,10258,openshift-cloud-controller-manager,...
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** `docs/stable/raw/aws-sno.csv` contains 6 ports no longer active on OCP 4.23 SNO: TCP 80/443 (router-default ingress pods not exposing EndpointSlices in SNO topology), TCP 8798 (MCD now internal on 127.0.0.1:8797), TCP 9192 (machine-approver kube-rbac-proxy port changed), TCP 9443/10258 (cloud-controller-manager-operator ports reorganized). These cause only `logrus.Warningf` calls (lines 108-110 and 154-155) and do NOT directly trigger the test failure, but the documented CSV needs updating for 4.23.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** Unknown (OCP 4.23 infrastructure-side changes removed/moved these services).
  - **Is it a flake?** No — these services are consistently absent on this 4.23 SNO cluster.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Update `docs/stable/raw/aws-sno.csv` to remove TCP 80, 443, 8798, 9192 and reconcile 9443/10258 entries to reflect the 4.23 SNO port layout.
  - **Affected tests:** "generated communication matrix should be equal to documented communication matrix" (warnings only, test passed).

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Groups 1 and 2 are independent — Group 1 is caused by a code regression in commatrix (PR #400 removed ephemeral range filtering), while Group 2 reflects OCP 4.23 infrastructure-side port changes that haven't been reflected in the documented matrix CSV. Group 2 doesn't cause test failure; Group 1 does.

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 4.23 (SNO/AWS): Both issues present; Group 1 is the direct failure cause.

**Affected Images:** `quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-v4.0-art-dev@sha256:c1cb03332d3a5e641eac1ae9732b0924a94a50d3b95866d112bb7c5a12cf0d51` (debug pod), `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.23.0-0.nightly-2026-06-25-142049`

**Overall Issue Class:** matrix_mismatch

**Overall Severity:** MEDIUM

28664217a2170569.title: the following ports are used but don't have an endpointslice:
28664217a2170569.ai_summary_short:
---

**TL;DR:** Ephemeral crio TCP ports slip through after PR #400 removed Linux dynamic-range filtering.

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **Group 1: Ephemeral crio ports no longer filtered by dynamic-range suppression** (affects: `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports`)
  - **Evidence:**
    ```
    FAIL: validation_test.go:161
    Fail("the following ports are used but don't have an endpointslice:
      Ingress,TCP,36893,,crio,,,master,false
      Ingress,TCP,39387,,crio,,,master,false
      Ingress,TCP,46841,,crio,,,master,false
      Ingress,TCP,40853,,crio,,,worker,false
      Ingress,TCP,45671,,crio,,,worker,false")
    ```
    From `raw-ss-tcp`: `LISTEN ... 192.168.17.198:39387 ... users:(("crio",pid=2848,fd=11))` — confirmed crio ephemeral binds, all in Linux range 32768–60999.
    From `matrix-diff-ss`: all five ports appear as `-` (open on node, no EndpointSlice), alongside filtered `rpc.statd` entries.
  - **Root Cause:** `crio` binds ephemeral TCP ports (32768–60999) for container networking; these have no EndpointSlice and are expected to appear transiently. Commit `7d56343` (PR #400, `shirmoran/remove-linux-dynamic-range`) removed the Linux ephemeral range from `commatrix.DynamicRanges`. The test at `validation_test.go:159` calls `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges(missingEPSMat, commatrix.DynamicRanges)` — without the Linux range in `DynamicRanges`, this filter no longer suppresses the crio ephemeral ports, so they reach the `Fail()` at line 161. The `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices()` (line 157) only skips `rpc.statd`, `dnsmasq`, and `dig` — `crio` is not excluded there either.
  - **Breaking PR/Commit:** `7d56343` — "Remove Linux Dynamic ranges from generated communication matrix" (merged via PR #400, `shirmoran/remove-linux-dynamic-range`)
  - **Is it a flake?** No — after PR #400, crio ephemeral ports will fail on every run (the specific port numbers change per reboot, but they are always present and always in the ephemeral range). The failure is systematic.
  - **Suggested Fix:** Add `"crio"` to the service skip-list in `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices()` in `validation_test.go`, since crio ephemeral ports are an expected transient OS-level artifact with no EndpointSlice. Alternatively, add a separate exclusion path for process-bound ephemeral ports without changing `DynamicRanges` semantics.
  - **Affected tests:** `Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports`

---

**Relation Between Groups:** Single root cause. The stale-in-matrix ports (TCP 8798 machine-config-daemon, TCP 9192 machine-approver, TCP 9443 cloud-controller-manager, UDP 68 NetworkManager — 6 total across master/worker) are logged as warnings via `notUsedEPSMat` at `validation_test.go:153–155` and do NOT trigger a test failure. They are a separate documentation hygiene issue: OCP 4.23 component changes shifted port 8798→8797 for MCD (confirmed in `raw-ss-tcp`: `machine-config-daemon` now listens on `127.0.0.1:8797`), and TCP 9443/9192 are no longer observed as open. These stale entries should be cleaned from `docs/stable/raw/bm.csv` but are not the cause of the CI failure.

**Per-Version Notes:** OCP 4.23 nightly (4.23.0-0.nightly-2026-06-25-142049): crio ephemeral port binding is consistently present on all nodes; dynamic-range suppression removed by PR #400 causes systematic test failure on every run.

**Affected Images:** `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/release:4.23.0-0.nightly-2026-06-25-142049` (cluster under test); `ci/telco-runner:latest` (test runner); `commatrix` cloned from `release-4.23` branch at commit including `7d56343`.

**Overall Issue Class:** `matrix_mismatch`

**Overall Severity:** HIGH

ad049d9d4b8d75b6.title: [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]
ad049d9d4b8d75b6.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** API server instability and a large linuxptp-daemon vendor sync (PR #613, merged 2026-06-23) together caused all 26 failures across 5 suites.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **8/26 — API server credential expiry + TCP unreachability (bc suite only):** 7 tests hit `Unauthorized` (kubeconfig credentials expired mid-run); 1 hit `dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout`; 1 cascaded to a suite-level timeout.
- **7/26 — linuxptp-daemon log stream never matched profile regex (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha):** `GetProfileLogID` / `GetForeignClockID` waited ~200–205 s for `Ptp4lConf: #profile: … message_tag` in the live log stream and got `context deadline exceeded`. The linuxptp-daemon pods never (re-)emitted the profile line, indicating the daemon (post-PR #613) does not re-log profiles on profile re-application as the tests expect.
- **6/26 — ptp4l.1 stuck in FREERUN, clockClass 248, never locked to GM (dualnicbc):** `GetClockClassViaPMC` consistently returned 248 instead of 6; the BC port re-elected itself MASTER after a bounce instead of recovering to SLAVE; cloud-event-proxy received no LOCKED events; PTP metrics were absent. The new `tBCTransitionCheck()` logic in the overhauled `pkg/daemon/daemon.go` (introduced in PR #613) is the primary suspect.
- **2/26 — T-GM fails to enter holdover (clockClass 248 instead of 7) after GNSS loss (tgm):** `DpllConfig.stateDecision()` never enters the `DPLL_HOLDOVER` branch; `getWorseState()` collapses a mixed HOLDOVER/FREERUN DPLL phase+frequency reading to FREERUN, so the event handler emits clockClass 248 instead of 7. Both tests see a deterministic wrong value, not a timing race.
- **2/26 — PMC command-format regression breaking BC master identity lookup (dualnicbc):** `finding pod for test-grandmaster: no PTP pods found for label="ptp/test-grandmaster"` — the new socket-based `pmc.go` introduced in PR #613 produces a command that the PMC binary does not parse, returning no output and leaving the grandmaster lookup empty.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Pattern | bc | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Log stream timeout (context deadline exceeded) | 2 | 3 | — | 2 | — |
| API credential expiry / TCP unreachable | 8 | — | — | — | — |
| clockClass 248 FREERUN (wrong state) | — | — | 6 | — | 2 |
| PMC command regression | — | — | 2 | — | — |

- **Shared across bc + dualfollower + dualnicbcha:** Log stream `context deadline exceeded` — the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet pods (`linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl`, `-qh745`, `-lrzfg`, `-fgzlj`, `-24cbs`) all fail to emit the expected profile log line. This is job-wide and cross-node, pointing at a daemon behavioral change rather than per-node hardware.
- **Shared across dualnicbc + tgm:** Incorrect clockClass 248 in contexts where 6 or 7 is expected. Different code paths but both trace back to the daemon's state-machine logic altered in PR #613.
- **Suite-specific:** API credential expiry is bc-only (likely a timing accident of which suite ran longest/first). PMC command regression is dualnicbc-only. T-GM holdover is tgm-only.
- **Common upstream trigger:** `linuxptp-daemon` PR #613 / commit `0459bc0` ("CNF-24996, OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream 22-Jun-2026", merged 2026-06-23) — a 5,947-file vendor sync that overhauled `pkg/daemon/daemon.go`, introduced `pkg/daemon/pmc.go`, and rewrote T-BC transition logic — is the strongest candidate for the majority of failures (Groups 2–5 above, covering ~17/26 tests).

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Revert or bisect linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (commit `0459bc0`).** It is the single most recent change to the daemon and plausibly breaks: profile log emission (17 tests across 3 suites), T-BC lock detection (6 tests in dualnicbc), DPLL holdover state resolution (2 tests in tgm), and PMC command formatting (2 tests in dualnicbc). File a blocker against the PR author to verify `printNodeProfile` / `applyNodePTPProfiles` still logs on each profile application, `tBCTransitionCheck` correctly reports LOCKED state, `pmc.go` emits a valid command string, and `getWorseState` correctly prioritizes HOLDOVER over FREERUN.

2. **Fix API server credential renewal in the telco5g CI cluster.** The 8 bc-suite failures caused by expired kubeconfig credentials and an unreachable `10.8.34.117:6443` endpoint are pure infrastructure. Ensure the CI job's service-account token or kubeconfig is rotated/refreshed before test execution, and verify the secondary API VIP `10.8.34.117` is included in load-balancer health checks.

3. **Add a pre-suite GNSS/DPLL readiness gate.** Before any tgm or dualnicbc test begins, assert that all relevant linuxptp-daemon pods have emitted their profile lines and that `ptp4l.1` has reached clockClass 6. This converts a silent 3-minute timeout into an immediate, actionable "cluster not ready" error and prevents cascading failures from a bad daemon state polluting unrelated test assertions.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_failure` (code regression in PR #613 is primary; `infra_other` secondary for API credential expiry)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 26/26 tests failed, every suite affected; the large vendor sync is a high-confidence regression candidate blocking the entire nightly 4.20 telco5g-ptp job.


1d6247377fc38805.title: [It] [bc-serial] PTP e2e tests Running with event enabled Event API version validation Should run event API v2 when apiVersion is explicitly set to 2.0 in PtpOperatorConfig [Serial]
1d6247377fc38805.ai_summary_short:
**TL;DR:** API server instability and a large linuxptp-daemon vendor sync (PR #613, merged 2026-06-23) together caused all 26 failures across 5 suites.

---

**Root Cause Groups:**

- **8/26 — API server credential expiry + TCP unreachability (bc suite only):** 7 tests hit `Unauthorized` (kubeconfig credentials expired mid-run); 1 hit `dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout`; 1 cascaded to a suite-level timeout.
- **7/26 — linuxptp-daemon log stream never matched profile regex (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha):** `GetProfileLogID` / `GetForeignClockID` waited ~200–205 s for `Ptp4lConf: #profile: … message_tag` in the live log stream and got `context deadline exceeded`. The linuxptp-daemon pods never (re-)emitted the profile line, indicating the daemon (post-PR #613) does not re-log profiles on profile re-application as the tests expect.
- **6/26 — ptp4l.1 stuck in FREERUN, clockClass 248, never locked to GM (dualnicbc):** `GetClockClassViaPMC` consistently returned 248 instead of 6; the BC port re-elected itself MASTER after a bounce instead of recovering to SLAVE; cloud-event-proxy received no LOCKED events; PTP metrics were absent. The new `tBCTransitionCheck()` logic in the overhauled `pkg/daemon/daemon.go` (introduced in PR #613) is the primary suspect.
- **2/26 — T-GM fails to enter holdover (clockClass 248 instead of 7) after GNSS loss (tgm):** `DpllConfig.stateDecision()` never enters the `DPLL_HOLDOVER` branch; `getWorseState()` collapses a mixed HOLDOVER/FREERUN DPLL phase+frequency reading to FREERUN, so the event handler emits clockClass 248 instead of 7. Both tests see a deterministic wrong value, not a timing race.
- **2/26 — PMC command-format regression breaking BC master identity lookup (dualnicbc):** `finding pod for test-grandmaster: no PTP pods found for label="ptp/test-grandmaster"` — the new socket-based `pmc.go` introduced in PR #613 produces a command that the PMC binary does not parse, returning no output and leaving the grandmaster lookup empty.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Pattern | bc | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Log stream timeout (context deadline exceeded) | 2 | 3 | — | 2 | — |
| API credential expiry / TCP unreachable | 8 | — | — | — | — |
| clockClass 248 FREERUN (wrong state) | — | — | 6 | — | 2 |
| PMC command regression | — | — | 2 | — | — |

- **Shared across bc + dualfollower + dualnicbcha:** Log stream `context deadline exceeded` — the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet pods (`linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl`, `-qh745`, `-lrzfg`, `-fgzlj`, `-24cbs`) all fail to emit the expected profile log line. This is job-wide and cross-node, pointing at a daemon behavioral change rather than per-node hardware.
- **Shared across dualnicbc + tgm:** Incorrect clockClass 248 in contexts where 6 or 7 is expected. Different code paths but both trace back to the daemon's state-machine logic altered in PR #613.
- **Suite-specific:** API credential expiry is bc-only (likely a timing accident of which suite ran longest/first). PMC command regression is dualnicbc-only. T-GM holdover is tgm-only.
- **Common upstream trigger:** `linuxptp-daemon` PR #613 / commit `0459bc0` ("CNF-24996, OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream 22-Jun-2026", merged 2026-06-23) — a 5,947-file vendor sync that overhauled `pkg/daemon/daemon.go`, introduced `pkg/daemon/pmc.go`, and rewrote T-BC transition logic — is the strongest candidate for the majority of failures (Groups 2–5 above, covering ~17/26 tests).

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Revert or bisect linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (commit `0459bc0`).** It is the single most recent change to the daemon and plausibly breaks: profile log emission (17 tests across 3 suites), T-BC lock detection (6 tests in dualnicbc), DPLL holdover state resolution (2 tests in tgm), and PMC command formatting (2 tests in dualnicbc). File a blocker against the PR author to verify `printNodeProfile` / `applyNodePTPProfiles` still logs on each profile application, `tBCTransitionCheck` correctly reports LOCKED state, `pmc.go` emits a valid command string, and `getWorseState` correctly prioritizes HOLDOVER over FREERUN.

2. **Fix API server credential renewal in the telco5g CI cluster.** The 8 bc-suite failures caused by expired kubeconfig credentials and an unreachable `10.8.34.117:6443` endpoint are pure infrastructure. Ensure the CI job's service-account token or kubeconfig is rotated/refreshed before test execution, and verify the secondary API VIP `10.8.34.117` is included in load-balancer health checks.

3. **Add a pre-suite GNSS/DPLL readiness gate.** Before any tgm or dualnicbc test begins, assert that all relevant linuxptp-daemon pods have emitted their profile lines and that `ptp4l.1` has reached clockClass 6. This converts a silent 3-minute timeout into an immediate, actionable "cluster not ready" error and prevents cascading failures from a bad daemon state polluting unrelated test assertions.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_failure` (code regression in PR #613 is primary; `infra_other` secondary for API credential expiry)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 26/26 tests failed, every suite affected; the large vendor sync is a high-confidence regression candidate blocking the entire nightly 4.20 telco5g-ptp job.

- **7/26 — linuxptp-daemon log stream never matched profile regex (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha):** `GetProfileLogID` / `GetForeignClockID` waited ~200–205 s for `Ptp4lConf: #profile: … message_tag` in the live log stream and got `context deadline exceeded`. The linuxptp-daemon pods never (re-)emitted the profile line, indicating the daemon (post-PR #613) does not re-log profiles on profile re-application as the tests expect.
- **6/26 — ptp4l.1 stuck in FREERUN, clockClass 248, never locked to GM (dualnicbc):** `GetClockClassViaPMC` consistently returned 248 instead of 6; the BC port re-elected itself MASTER after a bounce instead of recovering to SLAVE; cloud-event-proxy received no LOCKED events; PTP metrics were absent. The new `tBCTransitionCheck()` logic in the overhauled `pkg/daemon/daemon.go` (introduced in PR #613) is the primary suspect.
- **2/26 — T-GM fails to enter holdover (clockClass 248 instead of 7) after GNSS loss (tgm):** `DpllConfig.stateDecision()` never enters the `DPLL_HOLDOVER` branch; `getWorseState()` collapses a mixed HOLDOVER/FREERUN DPLL phase+frequency reading to FREERUN, so the event handler emits clockClass 248 instead of 7. Both tests see a deterministic wrong value, not a timing race.
- **2/26 — PMC command-format regression breaking BC master identity lookup (dualnicbc):** `finding pod for test-grandmaster: no PTP pods found for label="ptp/test-grandmaster"` — the new socket-based `pmc.go` introduced in PR #613 produces a command that the PMC binary does not parse, returning no output and leaving the grandmaster lookup empty.

---

**Cross-Suite Patterns:**

| Pattern | bc | dualfollower | dualnicbc | dualnicbcha | tgm |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Log stream timeout (context deadline exceeded) | 2 | 3 | — | 2 | — |
| API credential expiry / TCP unreachable | 8 | — | — | — | — |
| clockClass 248 FREERUN (wrong state) | — | — | 6 | — | 2 |
| PMC command regression | — | — | 2 | — | — |

- **Shared across bc + dualfollower + dualnicbcha:** Log stream `context deadline exceeded` — the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet pods (`linuxptp-daemon-4gvkl`, `-qh745`, `-lrzfg`, `-fgzlj`, `-24cbs`) all fail to emit the expected profile log line. This is job-wide and cross-node, pointing at a daemon behavioral change rather than per-node hardware.
- **Shared across dualnicbc + tgm:** Incorrect clockClass 248 in contexts where 6 or 7 is expected. Different code paths but both trace back to the daemon's state-machine logic altered in PR #613.
- **Suite-specific:** API credential expiry is bc-only (likely a timing accident of which suite ran longest/first). PMC command regression is dualnicbc-only. T-GM holdover is tgm-only.
- **Common upstream trigger:** `linuxptp-daemon` PR #613 / commit `0459bc0` ("CNF-24996, OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream 22-Jun-2026", merged 2026-06-23) — a 5,947-file vendor sync that overhauled `pkg/daemon/daemon.go`, introduced `pkg/daemon/pmc.go`, and rewrote T-BC transition logic — is the strongest candidate for the majority of failures (Groups 2–5 above, covering ~17/26 tests).

---

**Actionable Recommendations:**

1. **Revert or bisect linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (commit `0459bc0`).** It is the single most recent change to the daemon and plausibly breaks: profile log emission (17 tests across 3 suites), T-BC lock detection (6 tests in dualnicbc), DPLL holdover state resolution (2 tests in tgm), and PMC command formatting (2 tests in dualnicbc). File a blocker against the PR author to verify `printNodeProfile` / `applyNodePTPProfiles` still logs on each profile application, `tBCTransitionCheck` correctly reports LOCKED state, `pmc.go` emits a valid command string, and `getWorseState` correctly prioritizes HOLDOVER over FREERUN.

2. **Fix API server credential renewal in the telco5g CI cluster.** The 8 bc-suite failures caused by expired kubeconfig credentials and an unreachable `10.8.34.117:6443` endpoint are pure infrastructure. Ensure the CI job's service-account token or kubeconfig is rotated/refreshed before test execution, and verify the secondary API VIP `10.8.34.117` is included in load-balancer health checks.

3. **Add a pre-suite GNSS/DPLL readiness gate.** Before any tgm or dualnicbc test begins, assert that all relevant linuxptp-daemon pods have emitted their profile lines and that `ptp4l.1` has reached clockClass 6. This converts a silent 3-minute timeout into an immediate, actionable "cluster not ready" error and prevents cascading failures from a bad daemon state polluting unrelated test assertions.

---

**Overall Issue Class:** `test_failure` (code regression in PR #613 is primary; `infra_other` secondary for API credential expiry)

**Overall Severity:** CRITICAL — 26/26 tests failed, every suite affected; the large vendor sync is a high-confidence regression candidate blocking the entire nightly 4.20 telco5g-ptp job.
